# CDL gallery — the fig_01…fig_12 comparison on Sionna 38.901 CDL channels

This folder reproduces the synthetic-channel comparison gallery (`results/`) on a
**realistic 3GPP TR 38.901 CDL** channel generated by Sionna, instead of the
`RandomRayChannel`. Each figure is produced by a standalone
`scripts/cdl_fig_NN_*.py` wrapper that runs the matching `fig_NN_*` logic
(`scripts/figlib.py`) on a CDL channel (`scripts/cdllib.py`); every figure has a
per-figure `.md` here explaining what it shows and why.

Regenerate: `.venv/bin/python scripts/make_cdl_gallery.py` (add `--fast` for a
smoke test, `--model A` to pick a CDL profile). Requires the `sionna` extra.

## Channel configuration

* **Model:** CDL-C (rich NLOS), `--model {A,B,C,D,E}` to change it.
* **Carrier 3.5 GHz, 30 kHz SCS, 256-point grid, delay spread 100 ns.**
* **UE speed:** 3 km/h for the static figures; mobility (fig_05) uses 30 km/h
  with a 2 ms slot interval so the channel visibly ages over the horizon.
* **Array:** dual-pol `(4,2)` ⇒ P = 16 ports, N₃ = 8 (figures that sweep these
  state their own values). `n_rx = 2` (4 for fig_07).
* **Reproducibility/pairing:** Sionna's `generate()` is TF-driven and ignores
  the NumPy seed, so `cdllib.CDLReplay` seeds once, caches drops, and replays
  them (`reset()`) per scheme — every scheme and every channel-domain view is
  scored on the *same* CDL drops, paired and reproducible.

## How CDL differs from the synthetic channel

The qualitative story is unchanged — Type II tracks the eigen-subspace, Type I
trails (badly at rank 2), R17 wins after a PEB, R18 owns mobility. CDL mostly
**moves the numbers up**: CDL-C is spatially well-conditioned, so the Type II
families reach SGCS ≈ 0.95 (vs ≈ 0.91 on the sparse 4-ray channel) and sit even
closer to the bound. Two figures change character and are documented as such:

* **fig_04** uses no channel (pure spec bit-count formulas) — identical to the
  synthetic version.
* **fig_08 (left)** sweeps "number of rays", which has no CDL analog (the cluster
  structure is fixed by the model), so it is **flat**. The real channel-richness
  story lives in `results/sionna_cdl/cdl_models.png` (CDL-A…E sweep).

## Index

| Figure | What it compares |
|---|---|
| [fig_01](fig_01_se_vs_snr.md) | SE vs SNR, all families, ranks 1–2 |
| [fig_02](fig_02_rate_distortion.md) | fidelity vs feedback bits (Pareto plane) |
| [fig_03](fig_03_overhead_breakdown.md) | where each codebook spends its bits |
| [fig_04](fig_04_overhead_scaling.md) | overhead scaling laws (channel-independent) |
| [fig_05](fig_05_mobility.md) | CSI aging vs the R18 predicted PMI |
| [fig_06](fig_06_mu_mimo.md) | MU-MIMO ZF sum rate from reported PMIs |
| [fig_07](fig_07_rank_adaptation.md) | rank adaptation (fixed vs auto-RI) |
| [fig_08](fig_08_channel_sensitivity.md) | robustness to richness and estimation noise |
| [fig_09](fig_09_port_selection.md) | regular vs port-selection across domains |
| [fig_10](fig_10_array_scaling.md) | scaling with the antenna array |
| [fig_11](fig_11_frequency_granularity.md) | fidelity and cost vs N₃ |
| [fig_12](fig_12_summary.md) | scorecard radar across all axes |
